entry.title: MRM Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
entry.content:
Header

This blog is part of our series Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.

Explore the full series to learn how to streamline exchange rate management across your SAP ecosystem:

  • Intro: Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.
  • Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management in BTP
  • Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
  • Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud.

Introduction

Managing exchange rates is a critical part of any global business. SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) simplifies this process by providing automated updates for market data like exchange rates. In this blog, we’ll guide you through the detailed setup of MRM in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

 

What is SAP Market Rates Management?

SAP MRM is a cloud-based service that enables organizations to:

  • Retrieve market data such as exchange rates, interest rates, and indices.
  • Integrate this data into SAP S/4HANA Cloud or other SAP systems.
  • Use standardized providers like Refinitiv or custom upload options (BYOR - Bring Your Own Rates).

Prerequisites

Before setting up MRM, ensure you have the following:

  1. Access to BTP Cockpit: Admin rights to create and configure services in your BTP subaccount.
  2. Entitlement for Market Rates Management:
    • Navigate to Entitlements in your BTP subaccount.
    • Ensure the Market Rates Management - Basic or Advanced plan is available. If not, add it.

Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management

Follow these steps to configure MRM in BTP:

Step 1: Navigate to Your Subaccount

  1. Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
  2. Select your subaccount where you want to configure MRM.

 Step 2: Enable Entitlements

  1. Go to the Entitlements tab in your subaccount.
  2. Click Configure Entitlements.
  3. Add the Market Rates Management service:
    • Choose the appropriate plan (Basic or Advanced).
    • Save your changes.

Step 3: Create a Service Instance

  1. Navigate to Services > Instances and Subscriptions in your subaccount.
  2. Click Create to add a new service instance.
  3. Select Market Rates Management as the service.
  4. Choose the plan:
    • Basic Plan: Fetch exchange rates from predefined providers (e.g., ECB).
    • Advanced Plan: Supports additional features like historical data and advanced configurations.
  5. Assign a name to the instance and confirm.

Step 4: Generate Service Keys

  1. After the instance is created, click on the instance name.
  2. Select Create Service Key.
  3. Provide a meaningful name for the service key and generate it.
  4. Download or copy the service key JSON. It contains credentials like the API endpoint, client ID, and secret, which will be needed for integration.
    • We will need following information:
      • url – URL for OAuth authentication
      • clientid – username of API user
      • clientsecret – password of API user
      • downloadUrl – URL for download market rates information
      • uploadUrl – URL for upload own market rates information

Step 5: Test Connectivity

  1. Use the credentials from the service key to test the connection:
    • Open a tool like Postman or your integration platform.
    • Send a test request to the API endpoint provided in the service key.
  2. Verify that exchange rate data is successfully fetched.

 POST: <url>/oauth/token

POST: <downloadUrl>

 

Summary

Now that you’ve set up MRM in BTP, you’re ready to integrate it with your systems. In the next blog, we’ll cover how to build an iFlow in SAP Integration Suite to fetch and distribute exchange rates.

entry.title: The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series Starts Next Week!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we'll start the year with 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

 

Register here:

Artificial Intelligence & AnalyticsClick me!
Enterprise Portfolio and Project ManagementClick me!
Professional ServicesClick me!
Solution Orders and Subscription BillingClick me!
SalesClick me!
Cross Technology TopicsClick me!
Environment, Health, and SafetyClick me!
RetailClick me!
Supply ChainClick me!
SAP User ExperienceClick me!
Public SectorClick me!
ManufacturingClick me!
Sourcing and ProcurementClick me!
Governance, Risk, and ComplianceClick me!
ServiceClick me!
FinanceClick me!
Two-Tier ERPClick me!
ExtensibilityClick me!
Product ComplianceClick me!
Asset ManagementClick me!
SAP Activate, SAP Cloud ALM, Test Automation ToolClick me!

 

Sign-Up Instructions:

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1. Navigate to the sign-up page and click on the “sign up” button.​
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the live sessions and recordings through the SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
    ​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge) by clicking on "access".​

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Find out more about our live product expert sessions here.

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition - Key User and Developer Exensibility
entry.content:
Hello SAP Customers and Partners,

You may wish to influence SAP software and services to address your business needs and would like to see your ideas and feedback incorporated in the product. Since SAP's ticketing systems is meant only to address fixing of errors in S/4HANA Cloud, it is not the right channel to submit new feature requests to SAP. Instead, you may leverage our SAP Continuous Influence Sessions portal for this purpose.  

How to Open session and submit request:

  • Open link SAP Continuous Influence Sessions
  • Goto section ERP and Finance Products and click on 'SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition'.
  • New window will open then select 'SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Key User and Developer Extensibility session'.
  • You need S-User to submit and vote in Customer Influence portal, you need to accept respective terms and conditions once.
  • Here you submit one request per improvement ask, such as for a dedicated CDS view or SAP Fiori app.
  • Please check: Has a similar request already been raised? Use the "Search" functionality for your check. If available, make use of the voting mechanism otherwise submit new request.

Customers and Partners implementing and using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are entitled to submit improvement requests and vote.

  • You submit and vote for non go-live critical feature requests. 
  • Fast track process and 1 vote principle we follow in this continuous influence channel which is different than other SAP Continuous Influence channels.

Thank you for your continuous contributions. We look forward to continuing our journey with you in coming years. The complete list of all SAP Continuous Influence Sessions is available here.

entry.title: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps - Part 2
entry.content:
Introduction 

Custom movement types offer you the flexibility to individualize the movement of goods in your system to meet your specific business needs.  

Fiori apps that support custom movement types include: Goods Receipt for Production Order, Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery, Goods Receipt for Process Order, and Post Subsequent Adjustment. 

The implementation of custom movement types in your system is a 2-step process: 

  1. Use the configuration activity Copy of Movement Type (ID 103898) to Copy an Existing Movement Type. For more information on how to do this see the following blog post: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps 
  2. Implement the BAdI Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA.   

This blog will focus on step 2: the implementation of the BAdI Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts.

Prerequisites:  

  • An existing custom movement type. 
  • For the Custom Logic app: An Administrator or Extensibility Specialist role is required. 
  • For the Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app: An Inventory Manager or Warehouse Clerk role is required. 

Example Implementation  

Let's say that you need to use movement type 101 -post goods receipt - but want to post the goods receipt to a different account. You have already done the first step and copied an existing movement type to create a new movement type Z01. (See the example here: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps.) 

After creating your custom movement type, you must implement the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA to enable the use of your new movement type in one of the supported Goods Receipt Fiori apps. 

Here is how you can do this: 

Start by opening the Custom Logic app and clicking on Create in the table toolbar. 

From the Business Context drop-down, choose Inventory Management: Goods Receipt for Fiori apps PurOrd, ProdnOrd, NoRef, InbDeliv. 

Choose the Extension Point: Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts (MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE DATA) and choose Step 2.  

Enter an Implementation Description of your choosing. For this example, I will use CUSTOM_MVMT_TEST. The Implementation ID field will be filled automatically: YY1_ CUSTOM_MVMT_TEST. Choose Review 

Review the details. If everything looks good, choose Create.  

To add your specific code, choose Publish.

You can now click the Open Code Editor.  

In the code editing screen, you can now see a space to edit your code and another space for the parameters that are available for the BAdI. In the space for editing, you can choose to edit your implementation directly or, in this case, copy the sample code. To do this, you click on Show Sample Code: 

A pop-up appears. Choose Copy to Clipboard.  

After you copy the sample code, choose the Edit button. 

Paste the sample code into the editor. You will have to remove the first comment paragraphs and uncomment the rest of the code. (See below.) This example shows how you would enable your custom movement Z01 for use in the Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document Fiori app. 

 

Choose Save and Publish. 

Now that you have created this implementation, you can see your custom movement type in action. After you post a goods receipt for a purchasing document, you will see your custom movement type listed in the material document. 

As an added bonus, you can use the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_CHECK_DATA to implement your own validation logic for your custom movement type.  

For more details, see Custom Logic and App Extensibility: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document. 

I hope this blog helps you to create your own custom movement type implementation! If you found this information helpful and want to see more like it, please give this article a thumbs up!

entry.title: New Features for Key User Extensibility Transport Management in S/4HANA Cloud 3SL 2502
entry.content:
Introduction 

Did you ever need to manage the (parallel) implementation and maintenance of key user extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 3-system landscape? 

If the answer is ‘Yes’ you might have recognized that the Export Software Collection and Import Collection apps have some restrictions, which make it difficult to separate corrections from new development in the transport management process. 

In his blog post Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3SL (2): Parallel Implementation and Maintenance Thomas Schneider described the basic principles of parallel implementation and maintenance. In the following I will dive deeper into the process and highlight how new features in release 2502 help to overcome the restrictions for key user extensibility.

 

Working with Software Collections in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 3-System Landscape

With the introduction of the 3-system landscape, the process of transporting software collections for key user extensibility has changed significantly due to the introduction of the test system. But let’s look back at the 2-system landscape first, where key user extensions are created and tested in the quality system. After successful testing, they are exported with the next software collection version. After the export, the software collection version is ready for import into the production system.  

Figure 1: Transport Process in 2-System Landscape 

The example in Figure 1 shows two versions of the same software collection that were exported from the quality system after the release decision. They were imported to the production system afterwards. The ‘Changed (and Deleted) Items’ option is used first to export the newly developed Item 1 and Item 2. After that the ‘Hotfix’ option was used for the correction Item 1’ without exporting the changed Item 2’. This process is sufficient to manage parallel corrections and development in the 2-system landscape. 

Let’s look at the 3-system landscape now. In that landscape, key user extensions are created and tested in the development system. After that, they are exported to the test system for integration testing. The release decision on whether a version is ready for production is now made in the test system using the Import Collection app. However, the same rules for exporting and importing software collections are applied:  

  1. Dependencies are checked during the export of software collections. During the export, the Export Software collection app will always check the dependencies between key user items. With that all changes that are needed in production are contained in the same version or have been exported beforehand. 
  2. A higher version of the same software collection can’t overtake a lower version. The Import Collection app will make sure that the import happens in the same sequence as the export.  

The combination of both helps to reduce the risk of issues in production to a minimum. However, when doing parallel development and maintenance this leads to restrictions that are displayed in Figure 2 and Figure 3.  

Figure 2: Hotfix must include unwanted item 

Figure 2 shows an example where the hotfix was forced to include new development on Item 2’ because the corrected Item 1’ declares a dependency on it. Hence, new development Item 2’ would be imported to production with the hotfix. 

Figure 3: Rejected release decision blocks correction 

As shown in Figure 3, hotfixes can’t bypass lower versions containing new development. The release decision of the second ‘Changed (and Deleted) Items’ export has been declined. Hence, it blocks the immediate delivery of the subsequent hotfix.  

Both restrictions have been addressed by new features in release 2502 which will be described below starting with the dependency checks for the hotfix export. 

 

Dependency Checks for Hotfix Export 

Due to the dependency checks performed by the Export Software Collection app hotfixes often need to include content that isn’t part of the actual correction. 

Until to the 2408 release, the app checked which key user extensibility items are used by items contained in the hotfix. If the used items had the status ‘New’, ‘Changed’ or ‘Deleted’ they needed to be added to the hotfix. Otherwise, the export checks failed. 

In a 3-system landscape however, this led to an unwanted side effect when performing corrections and new development in parallel. If the item to be corrected used other items that were changed by new development, the app enforced the inclusion of these items into the hotfix. However, the new development might not have been ready for production, yet. 

With 2502 the checks for exporting software collection hotfixes have been reduced significantly. The dependency check for the hotfix export option has been changed in the 3-system landscape.  

Figure 4: Hotfix with relaxed dependency check 

As you can see in Figure 4 it’s no longer mandatory to include the used item 2’. The hotfix leaves Item 2 unchanged in test and production system. 

With that, it can be decided whether the change to a used item shall be contained in the hotfix or be exported in a later version. The Export Software collection app still helps to identify such dependencies. The check hasn’t been fully removed, but its severity has been reduced to a warning for items with the status ‘Changed’ or ‘Deleted’. However, it is still mandatory to include items with status ‘New’. Since new items have never been exported before, using such an item would cause import issues. Hence, they must still be included.  

 

Discarding Extensibility Collection Versions 

After exporting a hotfix and importing it to the test system, users encounter the next restriction in the 3-system landscape. The hotfix can’t be forwarded and imported to production until the same is done for all previous versions of the same software collection. 

This rule is part of the safety net for the key user extensibility transport process and prevents the production system from becoming inconsistent due to incorrect transport sequence. Until now, there was no possibility to ‘clear the way’ for corrections to the production system for key user extensibility.  

With 2502 key user extensibility collection versions can be discarded in the Import Collection app. 

Figure 5: Discarding software collections in test system 

As you can see in Figure 5 the ‘Discard’ action has been used after the declined release decision. The information about the discarded version was sent to the production system. After that it was possible to forward the hotfix and import it. 

The ‘Discard’ action has been available for other collection types (e.g., developer extensibility) already. The action changes the status of the collection version to ‘Discarded’ and prevents further actions. The result depends on the previous state of the collection version: 

  • Ready for Import: The collection version can’t be imported into the current system, nor can it be forwarded for import into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems (with status ‘Discarded’) so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. 
  • Imported (in test system only): The collection version can’t be imported into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. 

With that, software collection versions that block delivery of a correction to the production system can be ‘moved out the way’. However, this also means that the discarded collection versions can’t be forwarded to the production system later. The extensibility items on these versions need to be exported in a higher version of the same software collection to be consolidated to production later.  

Figure 6: Consolidating a discarded item 

In Figure 6 a software collection version was discarded to enable the hotfix for Item 1’’. However, the changed Item 2’ was not forwarded to production due to that. Another hotfix for the, now unchanged, Item 2’ was needed to consolidate it to the production system. 

The consolidation of discarded items can either be done with another hotfix for or during continued development, e.g. with another changed Item 2’’. However, there is the risk of missing some of the items that have been discarded. To counter that, the safety net for key user extensibility has been enhanced.  

 Figure 7: Consolidating an unchanged item 

As can be seen in Figure 7, Item 2 has never been consolidated to production system. During the next export of ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ Item 2 was included automatically.  

The Export Software Collection app checks the status of exported items in the production system. If a certain version of an item hasn’t reached the production system yet, the item is included in the next export when using the option ‘Changed and Deleted Items’, even when the items status is ‘Unchanged’. 

 

Conclusion

To manage corrections and new development occurring in parallel, the export option ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ should be used for new development, while corrections should be delivered via hotfixes. The relaxed export checks for hotfixes make it easier to separate corrections from new development, keeping the hotfix small. This ensures it can be imported to production without any side effects. 

The option to discard allows previous versions of the same software collection to be moved out of the way. However, the discarded collections can’t be imported to production later. Another version of the software collection will need to be exported to consolidate the discarded items, eventually. To ensure discarded items aren’t forgotten, the next export with ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ will automatically include those items, even if they are ‘Unchanged’. 

With these new features it becomes easier to manage parallel correction and development within a single software collection in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-system landscape beginning with release 2502. Please keep in mind that these features don’t apply to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2-system landscape. 

Additional Information and Links 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape | SAP Community 

Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public E... - SAP Community 

Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3SL (2):... - SAP Community

entry.title: Watch the Replays of Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we're hosting 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch the recordings on demand– anywhere, anytime.

Please note that, as the series is still ongoing, not all recordings have been uploaded to the SAP Learning Hub yet. Thus, resulting in a "no classes available" note when following some of the links.

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to 2025!

Our team has been hard at work, listening to your feedback and innovating to bring you a suite of new content. Whether you're navigating complex software enhancements, organizing release schedules, or simply ensuring your projects run smoother, these updates are crafted to keep you up to speed on the latest features. In this post, we’ll explore exciting new content provided by SAP experts, that is now at your fingertips and how it can significantly enrich your workflow.

Starting today, the content inside the Release Navigator will begin to be updated to 2502!

To reduce the impact on your user experience, starting after 2408, the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only includes new UX elements once a year, in August, when new release content is also updated.

Just a reminder, no tasks are required for the updates to the release navigator, as everything is automated. For more detailed information on product release dates, please refer to the Release Schedule card. Here you can find the UX innovation & content update plans for 2025.

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback or specific content requests for future updates to the Release Navigator. Please feel free to complete our feedback survey or contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post.

To explore more about how the Readiness@Scale team is dedicated to overseeing release readiness within the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio, visit our Release Ready page. Additionally, you can find all our team's blog posts by searching the tag  Readiness@Scale. Thank you for your continued engagement and support.

Best Run,

Readiness@Scale with SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 亮点前瞻：可拓展性
entry.content:
大家好，我们即将迎来 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 大版本的更新。在本篇博客中，我将会重点展示在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 当中可拓展性部分的亮点更新内容。

可拓展性对象变更概览

在本次的更新中，我们进一步丰富了支持的 CDS Views, BAdI 以及 API 的种类，以进一步满足公有云客户的客制化需求。在本次更新中，我们新增了302个 CDS Views，40种 BAdI ，30种 API 。更新覆盖了多个业务模块，在每一个业务模块可拓展性对象的使用都得到了大大增强，尤其是财务模块。例如，新的 BAdI BADI_FCLM_RCN_AUTOMATIC_MATCH，它可用于自动现金流对账的自定义规则。新的 OData V4 固定资产 API -主数据（API_FIXEDASSET）可用，您可以使用新的API读取、创建和更改主资产编号和子资产编号的资产主记录。

您可以通过该链接访问 What's New Viewer ，集中了解可拓展性对象在发生了哪些变更：https://help.sap.com/S4_CE_CHANGES_EXT_OBJ

 

自适应 UI 功能变更

到目前为止，可以基于经典主页中的组为新空间创建新页面。SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408版本已删除基于组的主页。在“维护业务角色”应用程序中创建新空间时，可以选择的“从业务目录和组创建页面”功能现已弃用，并将在未来的版本中删除。有关更多信息，请参阅使用主页内容创建空间和页面。

此外，现在当您自适应 UI 时，选择元素，上下文菜单是一致的。它不再取决于如何选择元素，例如使用鼠标左键或右键。当您左键单击元素时，熟悉的右键单击上下文菜单现在也会出现。

在本次版本升级后，“新增功能”对话框可让您随时了解最新的更新、功能和关键用户自适应方面的改进。对话框会自动显示新版本引入的新功能。如果您对以前版本的功能感兴趣，可以在工具栏的“更多操作”。

合作伙伴交付

在景观门户中定义 ABAP Namespace（请参阅Namespace）后，可以通过扩展性设置应用程序（请参阅使用关键用户扩展性开发扩展）启用ABAP命名空间，以便在 SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 系统中使用。之后，您可以在该名称空间中创建自定义字段，将它们传输到测试系统，并在合作伙伴加载项中交付自定义字段。

随着可扩展解决方案堆栈的普遍可用性，合作伙伴现在可以为客户构建和扩展解决方案，创造重要的价值并扩展功能。借助可扩展的交付，合作伙伴可以在 SAP S/4 HANA Cloud上开发ABAP Cloud 扩展，快速有效地覆盖更大的客户群，并将其作为附加产品提供给客户，客户可以在自己的系统环境中安装和使用这些附加组件。合作伙伴在自己的环境中和自己的 Namespace 下进行扩展的开发。经过全面测试后，解决方案将被注册并组装到一个可安装的包中。合作伙伴选择发布后，客户即可在 SAP Store 上使用该工具。客户可以使用 Discover Product 应用程序探索所有已购买和可用的产品，然后可以在其三系统环境中部署这些解决方案。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2024年01月 – 2024年03月）
entry.content:
本文主要收集了与集成扩展相关的常见问题(2024年01月 – 2024年03月)，按照主要问询对象API，CDS View，CPI，自定义字段和逻辑以及集成迁移相关对常见问题进行了大致的分类。客户可以点击下方链接查看到相关问题。

 

Contents

API相关问题... 1

CDS View相关问题... 2

自定义字段和逻辑相关问题... 2

集成相关问题... 3

其他问题... 3

 API相关问题

  • 问题：日记账分录的冲销按钮是否有API接口，客户在API Hub上没有找到。

解答：API Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)支持冲销操作。但是有一些限制：

只能冲销用API创建的日记账分录，在App里创建的日记账分录不能冲销。

已经创建后续文档的日记账分录不能被冲销。

已经清账或已经冲销的日记账分录不能被冲销。

预付款申请类型的不能被冲销。

模拟功能不支持。

 

  • 问题：如何使用API API_BATCH_SRV在storage location层面创建一个新的batch批次？

解答：在SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition里，batch level只能设为material层面，目前没有办法更新到storage location层面。如果有这个需求，可以提交CIP请求。

 

  • 问题：API接口无法读取数据，所有接口报401错误。

解答：这是由于客户使用的通信用户communication user太多失败的尝试too many failed attempts，导致用户被锁，所以所有的API调用读取数据失败。

 

  • 问题：使用新的Odata V4的purchase order的API，日期筛选条件跟之前V2的接口不一样，如何过滤LastChangeDateTime字段“2024-03-01”至“2024-03-15”的数据？

解答：可以按照以下的方式：

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrder?$filter=LastChangeDateTime ge 2024-03-01T00:00:00Z and LastChangeDateTime le 2023-03-16T00:00:00Z

 

  • 问题：是否有API可以实现由采购申请生成采购订单的相关操作？

解答：目前没有API可以实现把采购申请转换成采购订单，作为workaround，可以使用Schedule Purchasing Jobs - Advanced, 选择模板 "Automatic Creation of Purchase Orders from Requisitions"

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/669f0858cea31f60e10000000a44147b.html

 

  • 问题：使用接口 /sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetcreatemain 或 /sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetchange 创建或修改固定资产成功或失败都没有信息返回。

解答：因为是异步API，所以是正常行为。如果需要查看信息，可以去Message Dashboard查看。

 

 

CDS View相关问题

  • 问题：客户想要知道actual cost component和standard cost component的CDS视图，因为要开发报表用。

解答：目前没有一个标准的CDS视图，因为不同cost component的值不是直接存在DB，而是runtime计算的。下面这个链接是cost component view相关的CDS，供参考：

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/2a753666fbe5414aaf7bc80444581275.html?version=2308.503

 

 

  • 问题：CDS的primary source被deprecated了，但有多个其他CDS依赖于这个CDS。不能只替换primary data source而不产生影响，应该如何做？

解答：建议copy一份custom cds view，然后替换被deprecated data source。然后把原custom cds view替换为copied cds view。

 

  • 问题：自定义CDS在测试环境显示正常有金额，但在正式系统中不显示金额，全部为0。

解答：在自定义CDS中有如下一段：

cast(cast(YY1_VMI1_1_1.buhanshuidanjia as ABAP.INT1) * * YY1_VMI1_1_1.shuilvshuzhi as ABAP.INT1 ) as ABAP.DEC(20,2) )

在测试环境中：

=> YY1_VMI1_1_1.buhanshuidanjia  > 1 .

=> Therefore:  YY1_VMI1_1_1.buhanshuidanjia as ABAP.INT1 > 1

在正式系统中：

=> Therefore:  YY1_VMI1_1_1.buhanshuidanjia asABAP.INT1 = 0

=> Therefore: hanshuidanjia = 0

 

  • 问题：想要删除自定义CDS里的两个字段，可是删除按钮不可用。如何做才能删除？

解答：如果需要删除字段，需要把所有的相关性都移除。如何查找相关性：

  1. 进入Extensibility Inventory，搜索自定义CDS视图
  2. 在dependency tree里，查找哪些对象还有依赖性

也可以查看下面两个链接：

https://me.sap.com/notes/2997519

https://me.sap.com/notes/3095949

 

 

自定义字段和逻辑相关问题

  • 问题：客户不能删除某个BADI的draft，因为创建这个draft的用户已经离开公司了。但是，也已经不能以这个用户登录了。

解答：参考Note 3301760。如果之前的用户已经离开公司，那么开事件到组件BC-SRV-APS-EXT-BL，并提供希望把当前draft分配给哪个用户，SAP会帮忙解决。

 

 

集成相关问题

  • 问题：API地址：https://myxxxxxx-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/ 不能访问了，导致外部系统API调用都有问题。

解答：这是一个operation的问题，碰到类似问题可以提事件到XX-S4C-OPR-INC。同时，可以通过调用地址https://myxxxxxx.s4hana.ondemand.com/来调用API。

 

 

其他问题

  • 问题：过账购置（集成应付账款），做日常做待摊和办公固定资产资本化，升级后的系统中，找不到此APP。

解答：Post Acquisition (Integrated AP) - Without Purchase Order app (F-90) 在CE2308被标记为deprecated，在CE2402会被删除.

Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (MIRO) 是作为代替。

 

  • 问题：导入数据没有出错，但是中文文本跟导入文件里不符。

解答：当拷贝数据进XML文件时，不要选择copying data with formatting，应该只复制值。

 

 

 

 

以上为本次分享的全部内容。若想要了解往期更多有关集成扩展的常问问题，可以点击下方链接进行查看：

 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年01月 – 2022年03月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年04月 – 2022年06月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年07月 – 2022年09月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年10月 – 2022年12月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年01月 – 2023年03月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年04月 – 2023年06月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年07月 – 2023年09月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年10月 – 2023年12月)：click here.

entry.title: Creating a custom report in S/4HANA Cloud for Sales to display physical stock and sales order confir
entry.content:
In the logistical processes within an ERP system, we differentiate between physical (on-hand) stock on available stock. Physical stock is the total amount of inventory physically available in the supplying company’s plant, while available stock in simple terms represents the physical stock minus any quantities reserved for open transactions. The exact available quantity in the S/4HANA Cloud system is calculated using the mechanism of the so-called ATP (Available-to-promise) check. For a detailed overview on this topic, please refer to the following blog post.

With this introduction, we can take away that there are two stock quantities that are relevant when it comes to inventory management – physical quantity and available quantity. Within the S/4HANA Cloud system, these can be seen on material/plant level in a corresponding app – “Display Stock Overview” for the physical quantity and “Monitor Product Availability” for the available quantity. However, what happens when business users would like to see a consolidated view of the stock quantities across all materials in the system. This recently came up as a requirement in one of the projects as the customer previously had custom developed reports in an on-premise legacy system, which was used to display this aggregated data of both these quantities along with other relevant attributes. In this blog post, I would like to detail how a fairly similar result could be achieved using a Custom CDS View report in S/4HANA Cloud.

Step 1: Finding the CDS Views

In our case, this was the most challenging step. It is important to make sure that the CDS View to be used is released for key-user extensibility, such that it can be used for consumption in a Custom CDS View.

In our case, there were only limited CDS Views that fulfilled the exact data we were looking to display. To display the physical quantity, the View we decided on was I_StockQuantityValueByType. For the available quantity unfortunately, there is currently no CDS View which that exactly displays the available quantity details purely on material/plant level. The closest CDS View found was C_SalesDocumentSchedLineDEX_1, which provided the confirmed quantity with respect to the order quantity during the creation of a sales order item. This therefore doesn’t provide the total available quantity information as in the “Monitor Product Availability” app, rather shows whether the material was available in the desired quantity during the time of the ATP check.

Step 2: Creating the Custom CDS View

With the two Views selected above, we proceeded with the Creation of a Custom CDS View. Based on the individual requirements, this can be created as a Standard CDS View, analytical dimension or external API. Choose the following values for “Data Sources”, “Elements” and “Filter”:

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3: Publish the Custom CDS View and validate result

Now, we publish the Custom CDS View and can see how the results turn out.

 

In this excerpt for one example material, we can see the schedule line order and confirmed quantity during the time of the sales order creation. In this case, there was a fully confirmed schedule line, due to which the requested one piece was available and confirmed for the item 40 of sales order 93. Next to this, we see the physical quantity represented in the column “MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit”. As this is on storage location level, we therefore have a total physical quantity of 593 pieces in Plant 2901. As mentioned earlier, due to a lack of an exact CDS View to fetch the overall available quantity on material/plant level, only the sales order item specific schedule line quantities can be displayed. To have the exact available quantity as through the “Monitor Product Availability” app, a feasible approach would be to use the standard API for this to pull this data into a custom application on the BTP and display this as a report there.

Hopefully, this blog post gave an idea on how to use a custom CDS View report to achieve certain aggregations of data using embedded analytics, which have previously been achieved by custom development efforts in legacy systems.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2024年04月 – 2024年06月）
entry.content:
本文主要收集了与SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition集成扩展相关的常见问题(2024年04月 – 2024年06月)，按照主要问询对象API，CDS View，CPI，自定义字段和逻辑以及集成迁移相关对常见问题进行了大致的分类。客户可以点击下方链接查看到相关问题。

 

接口与集成相关问题

 

问题：客户想知道采购订单审批相关的API或CDS

解答：目前没有准确的相关的API或CDS，可以参考下面的CDS：

I_WorkflowStatusOverview

I_WorkflowStatusDetails

Scenario ID：WS00800238           Workflow Name：Workflow for Purchase Order

 

问题：采购申请API上如何添加组件的Subcontracting item，因委外采购申请需从外围系统发起，需做系统对接

解答：通过标准采购申请API无法添加组件

 

问题：客户想要知道是否可以通过API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV来更改supplier batch字段？

解答：当使用API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV做posting GR的时候，不支持输入supplier batch字段，因此不能通过API更新。

 

问题：用户需要S/4HANA Cloud消息推送与企业微信的集成

解答：可参考以下白皮书文档：

https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/qxvTx2x?ltr=a&rc=10&doi=SAP1078633

 

问题：采购订单备注字段的API接口如何获取

解答：通过以下接口可以获取采购订单明细的备注字段：

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/API_PURCHASEORDER_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrderItem/4500169906/0010/_PurchaseOrderItemNote

 

问题：客户在使用journal entry的SOAP API创建的时候出错，是关于自定义的字段出错。

解答：关于如何在journal entry日记账分录API中创建和使用自定义字段，可以参考以下博客文章：

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/guidelines-for-creating-and-enabling-extension-field-for-journal-entry-api/ba-p/13421678

 

问题：系统登录和使用非常慢，转圈，平均打开每个页面需要2分钟，外围系统接口交互异常，处理起来也频繁出错

解答：是由于数据库服务器上的memory issue，数据库自动恢复了，数据库和应用层的连接之后也恢复了。经过调查研究，memory issue是由于HANA DB的一个bug造成的，新版本的HANA DB已经修复此bug。

 

问题：使用接口 /sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetcreatemain 或/sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/fixedassetchange 创建或修改固定资产成功或失败都没有信息返回。

解答：因为是异步API，所以是正常行为。如果需要查看信息，可以去Message Dashboard查看。

 

问题：客户想要知道是否有接口可以新增工单组件？

解答：如果是三系统客户，可以使用以下接口做扩展开发：

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_PRODUCTIONORDERTP

但是，如果是二系统客户，确实没有这样的一个可用接口。

 

问题：客户想要通过API接口提取SAP内的所有会计凭证，例如包括费用报销、销售成本、生产成本、付款、收款等业务场景，想咨询应该使用API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM还是API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV？这两个有什么区别？

解答：API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM可以读取General ledger view的journal entry行项目；API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV可以读取entry view的journal entry，但是它不是用来抽取大量数据的。具体可参考以下两个链接：

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/80214b5ede2f4e97b640137a4eca208a.html?locale=en-US&q=API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/c024170fa7af40878975e218f3426387.html?locale=en-US&q=API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV

 

问题：客户通过自定义CDS的接口读取数据，发现返回数据特别慢。

解答：发现在系统慢的时候，同时有大量的并行的API调用API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV/A_BillOfMaterial，这些并行处理占据了大部分的会话进程，导致其他的调用非常慢。为了避免这个问题，推荐把这些调用绑定，而不是并行大量地同时调用。

 

问题：通过接口  /API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV/A_PurgInfoRecdOrgPlantData (PurchasingInfoRecord='xxx',PurchasingInfoRecordCategory='0',PurchasingOrganization='xxx',Plant='xxx') 来修改采购信息记录，payload如下：

{

    "IsMarkedForDeletion": false

}

但是报错：

error": {

        "code": "/IWBEP/CM_MGW_RT/022",

        "message": {

            "lang": "zh",

            "value": "Please check key fields of URI and payload"

        },

        "innererror": {

            "application": {

                "component_id": "MM-PUR-VM-REC",

                "service_namespace": "/SAP/",

                "service_id": "API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV",

                "service_version": "0001"

            },

            "transactionid": "335BF710CCFC0210E006645265FF29DA",

            "timestamp": "20240516135151.5639670",

            "Error_Resolution": {

                "SAP_Transaction": "For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details",

                "SAP_Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"

            },

解答：在payload中，除了要更改的字段，把key fields也放到payload中，修改后的payload如下：

{

    "PurchasingInfoRecord": "xxx",

    "PurchasingInfoRecordCategory": "0",

    "PurchasingOrganization":"xxx",

    "Plant":"xxx",

    "IsMarkedForDeletion": false

}

 

 

 

CDS视图相关问题

 

问题：税码对应的税率在哪个CDS或者API接口可以取到？

解答：在目前的版本中，没有可用的CDS可以取到税码对应的税率，用户需要使用SSCUI 101016来查看税码和税率。目前有以下类似的CIP请求，有类似需求可以打开链接投票：

CDS view for BSET table

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/231702

Release Used in Cloud Development for I_TaxCode

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/317901

Extension of the already existing CDS view I_TaxCode with the actual TaxRate

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/311388

 

问题：在自定义CDS视图中不能新建CDS视图

解答：首先第一，创建自定义CDS视图不能在P系统中做，必须在Q/D系统中新建之后再传输。

然后如果在Q/D系统中也不能新建，则需要去检查权限问题，是否有需要的角色和业务目录，在restriction里面的写入权限是不是un-restricted。

 

 

扩展开发相关问题

 

问题：对于科目类型为M/A/S的日记账分录行项目，自定义字段可以被更改。客户想要更改科目类型为D的行项目中的自定义字段，想问需要在业务上下文FINS_CODING_BLOCK或FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_ITEM中创建自定义字段吗？

解答：按照Note https://me.sap.com/notes/3448139 ， 增加的自定义字段只能针对科目类型为M/A/S的行项目更改或保存，不支持科目类型为D或者K的。如需实现功能，按照KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2963059  提交新功能。

 

问题：客户通过自定义业务对象自开发的App，在生产系统中找不到了，但是在测试系统中还是可以看到。

解答：这个通过自定义业务对象自开发的App，分配了业务目录SAP_CORE_BC_EXT，但是该业务目录已经弃用。需要把新的业务目录分配给自定义业务对象就可以解决该问题。

 

 

其他问题

 

问题：迁移主控室创建物料主数据提示错误“Virus scanner could not * *”

解答：virus scanner configuration里有问题，联系SAP operation团队帮助修复问题。

 

问题：导入产品主数据，发生错误"The custom property LANGUAGE is missing in the uploaded file."

解答：导致这个问题的原因是模板文件格式的问题，复制数据到模板里的时候，只拷贝值，不用复制格式等其他信息。

 

问题：客户的IAS打不开了：https://XXX.accounts.ondemand.com/admin/

解答：使用新连接：

https://XXX.accounts.sapcloud.cn/admin

 

问题：使用App Mass Changes to Purchasing Info Records来修改物料的采购信息记录的价格（PPR0）时，根据模板导入，但是系统只更新了第一条记录的价格，其他的记录没有更新成功。

解答：如何通过导入Excel文件的方式来修改采购信息记录的价格，可参考以下链接：

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/offline-price-update-for-purchasing-info-record-via-microsoft-excel/ba-p/13538542

 

问题：是否有方法可以进行批次的批量转移？（Mass transfer stock from batch to batch）

解答：目前没有app或transaction可以做mass goods movement posting。在MIGO/Post Goods Movement里，选择Detail Data section，然后可以复制/粘贴物料、plant等信息。但是不能通过导入Excel文件的方式来实现批量操作。

 

 

 

 

以上为本次分享的全部内容。若想要了解往期更多有关集成扩展的常问问题，可以点击下方链接进行查看：

 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年01月 – 2022年03月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年04月 – 2022年06月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年07月 – 2022年09月）：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2022年10月 – 2022年12月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年01月 – 2023年03月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年04月 – 2023年06月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年07月 – 2023年09月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2023年10月 – 2023年12月)：click here.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud集成与扩展FAQ（2024年01月 – 2024年03月)：click here.

entry.title: Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and interested in requesting and learning more about how the available CQC services can support your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution? If so, then I invite you list to our below E-learning.

  • Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

(Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.)

Service Overview:

ServiceAimWhen to UseAdditional Information
CQC for Transformation AssessmentGauge/provide feedback on your project team’s awareness of topics and resources that are critical to project success

Prepare session in prepare phase

Realize session in the realize phase

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Deployment ReadinessPre go-live SAP best practice review of key SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition settings

Two months prior to go live

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Process Adoption

Service will review the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution and help you improve current business processes and adopt new business processes.

Run phase at least 6 months after go-live
  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Data Volume Management

Review the solution under data volume management and information lifecycle management aspects

Run phase at least 6 months after go-liveIn development – if interested please reach out to your SAP Contact

All CQCs listed above are included as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition support maintenance contract which is embedded in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription with no additional costs. Please note you can also find a complied list of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learnings here - SAP Enterprise Support Learning Assets for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Meet-the-Expert Sessions and E-Learnings.

In addition, please sign up for the value map for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to receive full guidance on CQCs and get personalized guidance on your cloud ERP journey. In the value map, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or use the “Request a Call” feature to speak with an expert. To learn more about value maps, watch the following video.

entry.title: In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2025 公有云深度赋能系列合集
entry.content:
从产品专家到产品专家

在云ERP产品成功团队，我们始终致力于使我们的客户和合作伙伴充分利用SAP S/4HANA 公有云版的全面解决方案，并从他们的投资中获得最大价值。

通过我们的“SAP S/4HANA 公有云版深度解读”计划，我们提供一系列深入的专题会，分享产品专家的知识，详细解释SAP S/4HANA 公有云版的新功能和特性以及其为业务带来的创新价值。

entry.title: 探索SAP扩展架构指南：开启企业定制化之路
entry.content:
在当今快速变化的商业环境中，企业需要灵活应对各种挑战和机遇。为了实现业务流程的定制化和扩展，SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide）为企业提供了一个决策框架。然而，许多企业在选择合适的SAP技术时面临困惑，不知道如何在不同场景下做出最佳决策。缺乏清晰的指导可能导致资源浪费和效率低下。那么，SAP扩展架构指南如何帮助企业在复杂的技术环境中做出明智的决策，实现业务流程的定制化和扩展呢？

什么是SAP扩展架构指南？

SAP扩展架构指南是SAP BTP指导框架的一部分，旨在帮助企业架构师和解决方案架构师理解SAP的可扩展性模型，并提供全面的决策指导。通过该指南，企业可以了解如何在SAP S/4HANA Cloud私有云版本、SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本和SAP S/4HANA与SAP业务技术平台（BTP）结合使用的环境中开发扩展。

为什么选择SAP扩展架构指南？

  • 决策支持：指南提供了一个清晰的决策框架，帮助企业在不同的技术选项中做出明智的选择。
  • 灵活性：企业可以根据自身的业务需求，选择合适的扩展模式，包括堆栈内扩展和并行扩展。
  • 未来准备：指南确保企业的扩展方案具备云就绪性和未来的弹性，帮助企业在不断变化的市场中保持竞争力。

如何使用SAP扩展架构指南？

  • 理解扩展模式：指南详细介绍了SAP S/4HANA各种版本的堆栈内扩展和SAP BTP的并行扩展模式，帮助企业根据具体需求选择合适的扩展方式。
  • 应用扩展方法论：指南基于SAP应用扩展方法论，使用其概念和术语，帮助企业开发符合核心合规要求的扩展。
  • 决策支持工具：通过指南提供的决策支持工具，企业可以更有效地分析和评估不同的扩展选项，确保选择最佳的技术方案。

扩展使用案例模式

在实际应用中，企业可能需要结合堆栈内扩展和并行扩展来满足业务需求。以下是一些常见的SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本扩展使用案例模式：

堆栈内扩展

堆栈内扩展通常需要与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本的数据、事务或应用程序紧密耦合。以下是一些典型的堆栈内扩展特征：

  • 事务一致性：当需要同时更改SAP CDS视图和字段的数据以及自定义表和字段的数据时，事务一致性是必需的。
  • 高数据读取量：需要复杂的SQL查询和连接来读取高数据量。
  • 频繁访问和更改数据：需要频繁访问和更改SAP CDS视图和字段的数据。

实例 - 某制造企业的预付款申请流程： 客户需求是按采购订单申请预付款，并打印请款单后同步至OA进行审批，OA审批通过后生成往来付款凭证，另外客户要对申请金额进行卡控。

  1. 供应商编号选择：在进行预付款申请时，需要选择多个供应商进行付款。通过Value Help窗口，用户可以快速选择并添加多个供应商编号，确保付款申请的准确性和效率。
  2. 计划付款时间查询：需要根据不同的付款计划时间进行查询，以便安排资金流动。用户可以通过设定时间范围，快速筛选出符合条件的付款申请单。
  3. 创建预付款申请单：需要为新供应商创建预付款申请单。用户选择供应商后，系统会弹出预付款申请单页面，用户可以填写相关信息并提交申请。
  4. 提交审批：在完成预付款申请单的填写后，点击提交审批按钮，系统会提示用户确认提交，并将申请单状态修改为审批中，等待上级审批。
  5. 打印请款单：在预付款申请单提交审批后，需要打印请款单以备存档。用户选择预付款申请单后，系统会弹出PDF预览窗口，用户可以查看并打印请款单。
  6. 删除预付款申请单：在发现预付款申请单填写错误，需要删除重新申请。用户选中状态为处理中的单据后，点击删除按钮，系统会删除该申请单。
  7. 查看申请单明细：需要查看某预付款申请单的详细信息。用户点击数据行后，系统会弹出该申请单的详细页面，显示所有相关信息，方便用户进行审核和管理。

并行扩展

并行扩展可以通过API和事件与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本解耦。以下是一些并行扩展的特征：

  • 松耦合：通过API和事件与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本解耦。
  • 灵活性：可以独立于SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本进行开发和部署。

实例 - 某制造企业的采购申请流程： 客户需求是将采购申请同步至钉钉OA进行审批，并在审批通过后生成相关凭证。此外，客户需要对采购申请的状态进行实时监控。

  1. 采购申请同步：为了能够将采购申请同步到钉钉OA，必须在SAP中定期维护钉钉部门ID和人员ID。需要同步的采购申请类型包括Z1、Z2、Z3、Z4、Z5和Z6。
  2. 采购申请审批状态同步：未打删除标记的采购申请行项目会被同步。如果所有行项目都已标记为删除，则该采购申请不会同步。在钉钉OA审批过程中发生错误时，审批状态码为99。如需重新执行审批结果同步程序，请手动将审批状态按钉钉的状态重新设置。
  3. 程序和URL：文档列出了用于同步和更新采购申请数据的各种程序及其URL。
  4. 问题和决策：文档还提到了一些在集成过程中遇到的问题和做出的决策，例如取消物料主数据和供应商主数据的集成。

结论

SAP扩展架构指南为企业提供了一个强大的工具，帮助他们在复杂的技术环境中做出明智的决策。通过理解和应用该指南，企业可以实现业务流程的定制化，提升竞争力，并为未来的发展做好准备。

扩展体系结构指南基于SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide） 并使用其概念和术语。了解如何使用 SAP S/4HANA 和 SAP Business Technology Platform 开发精简的核心合规扩展。有关详细信息，请参阅SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide） 文档。

entry.title: How to implement additional restrictions for users creating Sales Orders
entry.content:
If you want to restrict users to create sales orders, you can use the provided restriction types in the business catalog (see help portal). 

If that types are not sufficient for your use case, you can implement additional checks using key user extensibility. In this blog, we will use key user extensibility to restrict users for specific plants and give a demo for this function.

For this function, we create the following Custom Business Object in the Custom Business Object app:

- YY1_PLANTRESTRICTION: This business object could set the restrictions for plants for different user.

 

In this Custom Business Object, in the subpage General Information, we need to select some attributes to meet our requirement. For more details, you could refer to Custom Business Objects | SAP Help Portal.

In the subpage Fields, we will add two fields called Username and Plant.

For these two fields, we use Association to CDS View as a type. With that we get a value help for available users and plants in the system.

For field Username, we use value help view I_WORKFORCEPERSONVH.

For field Plant, we use value help view I_PLANTSTDVH.

After maintaining fields, we could publish this Custom Business Object for the next step. And then maintain data in “Maintain Catalogs” from subpage General Information. If you do not maintain any catalogs, the “Generated UI” will not enable to be opened for lacking of authority.

For our example, we maintain this catalog (Extensibility – Custom Business Objects) for Business Object (PlantRestriction). Then the user who own this catalog could be able to maintain the value for Business Object "PlantRestriction".

After maintaining the catalog, you can navigate to the generated UI from subpage General Information.

In this UI, you could create, update and delete any user and plant restriction you like. Here I configured two users’ restriction. User Wesley can only use plant 1010 and Yilin can only use plant 1710.

Then we need to implement the cloud BADI SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE to prevent mentioned user to use the restriction plant.

 

 

IF salesdocument-purchaseorderbycustomer = 'Plant Check'.
    DATA(lv_actual_user_alias) = cl_abap_context_info=>get_user_alias(  ).

    LOOP AT salesdocumentitems INTO DATA(lv_salesdocumentitem).
        SELECT COUNT(*)
          FROM yy1_plantrestriction
          WHERE plant = _salesdocumentitem-productionplant
          AND username = _actual_user_alias.

        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
            DATA(lv_error_message) = |You are not allowed to use plant| & | | & |{ lv_salesdocumentitem-productionplant }|.
            APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E' messagetext = lv_error_message ) to messages.
            EXIT.
        ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

 

 

 

After all of the configuration, then you could verify it in the system. We use Yilin as a login user.

Then use this user to create a sales order with plant 1710, you will get the error message you write in the BAdI.

 

Additional Hint:
If you want to receive the error message immediately after adding or changing an item and not only at save, you could implement similar logic in BAdI SD_SLS_CHECK_ITEM.

entry.title: How to Add a Dynamic Custom Section with Custom Fields to the "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2" App
entry.content:
Overview

In this blog post, we will show how to add an additional section called Custom Fields to the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app. This section appears dynamically based on the sales order data. For this blog post, we will use the sales organization as criterion, but you can also implement the logic based on other standard or custom fields.

We use both key user extensibility and developer extensibility for the implementation:

Key User Extensibility

1. In the Custom Fields app, we create a calculated field to manage the visibility of the custom section.

Developer Extensibility

2. In ABAP Development Tools (ADT), we use the Extensibility Wizard to create several custom fields.

3. We adjust the CDS annotation of the newly created extension view for C_SALESORDERMANAGE.

Testing

4. In the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app, we check if the implementation works.

Let's get started!

 

Implementation Steps

1. Creating a Calculated Field

  1. In the Custom Fields app, choose Data Source Extensions, choose New, and enter the following information:
  2. In the detail view of the data source extension, enter a BAdI implementation name, and add a new calculated field of type Boolean.
  3. Choose Create.
  4. Choose Save and then Publish. 
  5. When the data source extension is published, navigate back to the details of the newly created data source extension. You will see a link to the implementation of the calculated field:
  6. Choose the link to open the code editor and enter the following code sample:

 

LOOP AT it_entity_data REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_entity_data).
 READ TABLE ct_calculated_fields REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_calculated_fields) INDEX sy-tabix.
   IF lr_entity_data->salesorganization = '1710'.
     lr_calculated_fields->yy1_myuicontrolfield = abap_false.
   ELSE.
     lr_calculated_fields->yy1_myuicontrolfield = abap_true.
   ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

 

 g. Choose Save and Publish.

As you can see in the code sample, we only want to display the custom fields for sales organization 1710, not for any other sales organizations.

2. Creating Custom Fields

We now create the custom fields that we want to be displayed in the new section. 

  1. Open your ADT and search for the C_SALESORDERMANAGE data definition. Right-click C_SALESORDERMANAGE and select Generate ABAP Repository Objects:
  2. Select ABAP Cloud: Extensibility -> Extension Fields and then choose Next.
  3. Choose a package for your development and choose Next.
  4. In the next step, you can add some custom fields in the first section.
    The second section displays all the extensions that will be created in the business object layer.
    The third section lists all the business object layer extensions that will be created.

    As you started from C_SALESORDERMANAGE, it is pre-selected, but you can also select the OData V4 API for sales orders (A_SALESORDER_2) to create an additional extension for that service. In our scenario, we will focus on the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app (C_SALESORDERMANAGE), and we have created two custom fields with standard data elements. You could also create new data elements in advance and use them here.
  5. Choose Next. A preview of the artifacts to be created will be displayed: 
  6. Choose Next, select a transport request, and then choose Finish. The artifacts are created, and you can find them in your package in the Project Explorer.

3. Adjusting the CDS Annotation

  1. Open the extension for C_SALESORDERMANAGE and adjust the content as follows:

 

extend view entity C_SalesOrderManage with
{
  .facet: [ { id: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 label: 'Custom Fields',                 
                 type: #COLLECTION,
                 position: 65,
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 hidden: #(YY1_MyUIControlField) },
               { id: 'CustomFieldsLeft',
                 label: 'Custom Fields Left',
                 type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
                 parentId: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 position: 10,
                 targetQualifier: 'CustomFieldsLeft' },
               { id: 'CustomFieldsRight',
                 label: 'Custom Fields Right',
                 type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
                 parentId: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 position: 20,
                 targetQualifier: 'CustomFieldsRight' } ]

  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'CustomFieldsLeft', position: 10, importance: #HIGH } ]
  @EndUserText.label: 'My Custom Field 1'
  SalesOrder.ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD1_SDH as ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD1_SDH,

  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'CustomFieldsRight', position: 10, importance: #HIGH } ]
  @EndUserText.label: 'My Custom Field 2'
  SalesOrder.ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD2_SDH as ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD2_SDH
}

 

Tips:

  • Line 6: We have chosen position 65 to place the custom fields between the Texts and Status and Blocks sections on the UI. You can find all positions of the other sections in C_SALESORDERMANAGE (Metadata Extension) if you want to have the custom fields in a different position.
  • Line 8: The calculated field name YY1_MyUIControlField is case-sensitive.
  • Line 8: Do not use spaces inside the brackets.
  • Lines 3, 12, 19: To create subsections, assign them via the parentId.
  • Lines 15, 22, 24, 26, 28, 30: To assign custom fields to a specific facet, use the (target)Qualifier.

4. Checking Your Implementation

In the Manage Sales Order - Version 2 app, create a sales order for sales organization 1710 and check if the Custom Fields section is displayed as follows:

Let's create a second sales order for sales organization 1010 and check if the Custom Fields section is displayed.

You can see from the above screenshot that the Custom Fields section is not displayed for sales organization 1010.

entry.title: Custom Substitution Functions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
To replace the values of Journal Entry fields that are not available as parameters in substitution BAdIs, substitution rules can be used in the app 'Manage Substitution and Validation Rules'. Although substitutions with constant values or values obtained from Custom Business Objects (table lookup) can be handled solely in the app, when standard CDS views are needed as the data source, a custom substitution function should be created in ABAP environment.

Use

As an example, to substitute the sold product value in the profitability segment with the product of the related sales document item, first an ABAP class that implements the interface if_fin_re_custom_function needs to be generated. 

Inside the method get_parameters, function parameters are defined in an internal table with a unique name and a data type which aligns with the source fields, in this case sales document and sales document item.

 

 

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_parameters.
    rt_parameters = VALUE #(
    ( name = gc_parameter_1 abap_type = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'VBELN_VA' ) ) )
    ( name = gc_parameter_2 abap_type = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'POSNR_VA' ) ) )
      ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

The method is_disabled determines the visibility of the substitution function based on an event ID and its used to make the function available for a specific event . In this case, disabling the function in all cases other than the event ID 'FINS_ACC_MS_1' assures that the function is only visible for rules with the business context 'Market Segment'.

 

 

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~is_disabled.
    IF iv_event_id = 'FINS_ACC_MS_1'.
      rv_disable = abap_false.
    ELSE.
      rv_disable = abap_true.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

The return type of the function is aligned with the target field, the type of the field product for the case.  

 

 

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_returntype.
    ro_type =  CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'ZCO_CL_DERV_CO_PRD=>GTY_PRD' ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Inside the method execute, function parameters are read, the target field is obtained with a query, and set as the return value. In this case, the product of the related sales document item is gathered and then returned. (Only constants or static parameters will actually substitute the target field. ).

 

 

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~execute.

    READ TABLE is_runtime-parameters WITH KEY p COMPONENTS name = gc_parameter_name INTO DATA(ls_vbeln).
    READ TABLE is_runtime-parameters WITH KEY p COMPONENTS name = gc_parameter_2 INTO DATA(ls_posnr).

    IF ls_vbeln-value IS BOUND AND ls_posnr-value IS BOUND.

      DATA(lv_vbeln) = CONV vbeln_va( ls_vbeln-value->* ).
      DATA(lv_posnr) = CONV vbeln_va( ls_posnr-value->* ).
      CHECK lv_vbeln IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_posnr IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT SINGLE FROM i_salesdocumentitem WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
      FIELDS Product
      WHERE salesdocument EQ _vbeln
        AND salesdocumentitem EQ _posnr
      INTO (lv_prd).

      gv_prd = lv_prd.
      ev_result = REF #( gv_prd ) .
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 Entire class:

 

 

CLASS zco_cl_derv_co_prd DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_fin_re_custom_function .
    TYPES:    gty_prd TYPE matnr.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA: gv_prd TYPE matnr.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CONSTANTS: gc_function_name  TYPE fin_re_custom_function_name VALUE 'ZCO_DERIVE_PRD',
               gc_parameter_1 TYPE c LENGTH 5 VALUE 'VBELN',
               gc_parameter_2    TYPE c LENGTH 5 VALUE 'POSNR'.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCO_CL_DERV_CO_PRD IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~check_at_rule_activation.
    ev_rc = if_fin_re_custom_function=>rc-ok.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~execute.
    READ TABLE is_runtime-parameters WITH KEY p COMPONENTS name = gc_parameter_name INTO DATA(ls_vbeln).
    READ TABLE is_runtime-parameters WITH KEY p COMPONENTS name = gc_parameter_2 INTO DATA(ls_posnr).

    IF ls_vbeln-value IS BOUND AND ls_posnr-value IS BOUND.
      DATA(lv_vbeln) = CONV vbeln_va( ls_vbeln-value->* ).
      DATA(lv_posnr) = CONV vbeln_va( ls_posnr-value->* ).
      CHECK lv_vbeln IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_posnr IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT SINGLE FROM i_salesdocumentitem WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
      FIELDS Product
      WHERE salesdocument EQ _vbeln
        AND salesdocumentitem EQ _posnr
      INTO (lv_prd).


      gv_prd = lv_prd.
      ev_result = REF #( gv_prd ) .
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_description.
    rs_msg = VALUE symsg( msgid = 'ZCO' msgno = '001' ).
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_name.
    rv_name = gc_function_name.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_parameters.
    rt_parameters = VALUE #(
    ( name = gc_parameter_1 abap_type = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'VBELN_VA' ) ) )
    ( name = gc_parameter_2 abap_type = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'POSNR_VA' ) ) )
      ).
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_returntype.
    ro_type =  CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'ZCO_CL_DERV_CO_PRD=>GTY_PRD' ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_return_valuehelp.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~is_disabled.
    IF iv_event_id = 'FINS_ACC_MS_1'.
      rv_disable = abap_false.
    ELSE.
      rv_disable = abap_true.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

After the class is activated, inside the app "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" a substitution rule with the related business context is created, and a target field is selected with the type "Substitute with Field/Function". 

The created function will appear with the set name and the description.

The function parameters are binded to the related source fields. ( FIN_SUBVALPROFSEGMENTLINEITEM~SalesDocument & FIN_SUBVALPROFSEGMENTLINEITEM~SalesDocumentItem for the case ).

After enabling the rule, the custom function will be executed by the related business process, and the derived value will substitute the target field.

Reference

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cf92618cf8f849999fc08a03dc53c1a5.html

entry.title: Creating a CBO with CRUD operations | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction
Creating a Custom Business Object (CBO) in Public Cloud, from the initial setup to enabling CRUD operations. This includes communication scenario, arrangement, and catalog association. A simple process to enable customizations in the environment.


Step 1 - Creating the CBO (Custom Business Object)
Access the Custom Business Object app.



Create a new CBO.

 

 

 


To enable future CRUD access, select this option.
Add the required fields, define data types and keys, then click Publish.


Step 2 - Creating the Communication Scenario
Access the Custom Communication Scenarios app.


Create a new Communication Scenario.

 

 




Define a name and description.

Save and publish.





Step 3 - Creating the Communication Arrangement
Access the Communication Arrangements app.



Create a new Communication Arrangement.

 

 




Associate it with the previously created Communication Scenario.



Select the Communication System (e.g., ZLOCAL, already pointing to the environment).

 

 




Publish.


Step 4 - Linking Communications to the CBO
Open the previously created CBO.
In the Inbound Services tab, add the created service.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 



The Communication Arrangement is now associated.

 



Step 5 - Assigning the CBO to a Business Catalog
Access the Custom Catalog Extensions app. 



Locate the two objects that need to be linked to a Business Catalog (IBS and SCBO).

 






 

 



 

 




Publish.


Step 6 - Accessing the CBO CRUD (SM30 Equivalent)
1- Accessing through the CBO. Click in Go To Generated UI

 



2- Searching via apps.

 



Step 7 - Result
Click in Create to create a record.

entry.title: Releasing "requests" | In-App Mode (Custom Objects) | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In Public Cloud, the customization transport process differs from Private Cloud (STMS). The request is now called Collection. When transporting objects in In-App mode, each modification generates a new version within the Software Collection. You can add only the modified items, include comments, and use filters to streamline the search process. After importing the collection into the Test environment, performing the Forward action is necessary to make it available for import into PRD.

Step 1
Access the Export Software Collection app.



Create a Software Collection (if one does not already exist for the relevant topic or module/organization in the environment).


Name your Software Collection and click Create.

Once the Software Collection is created, note that it is versioned. This means that with every object modification and transport, a new version is automatically created for transport.




In Add Items, you can select the objects to be included in this Collection for transport.

 

 

 


Support Filters are available to assist with object searches within the environment.



After adding the desired objects, click Check to verify if there are any blockers, dependencies, or inconsistencies for transport.


Check Option
Note: It is possible to transport only the modified items or all items again. The first option is always recommended.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Export Option – This is where the release (Export) to the Test environment actually takes place.

 

A comment can be added for each versioned transport.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.

 

 

 

 

 




Step 2
After exporting, log in to the Test environment and access the Import Collection app.


Use the available filters to search for the desired collection. Upon reaching the Test environment, the versioned collection will have the status "Ready for Import."

 

 

 

 

 


Select the desired versioned collection and choose Check and Import.

 

 

 

 

 



Add a transport comment.



Click Manual Import.

The import process starts, and the collection status changes to "Importing" during processing.


Once the import is complete, a confirmation message is displayed:


Step 3
The collection has only been imported into the Test environment but is not yet available for import into the Production environment. To make it available for import into PRD, it is necessary to perform the Forward action.

 

 

 

 



Select the collection and click Forward.




The collection is forwarded to the Production environment transport path.

 

 

 

 

 


To finalize, log in to the Production environment and access the Import Collection app.




Select the desired collection and import it.



The collection has been successfully imported into the PRD environment.

entry.title: Transportando "requests" | Modo In-app | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introdução

Em Public Cloud, o processo de transporte de customizações é diferente de como fazemos em Private Cloud (STMS). A antiga request agora é chamada de Collection. Ao transportar objetos no modo In-App, cada modificação gera uma nova versão dentro de uma Software Collection. É possível adicionar apenas os itens modificados, incluir comentários e usar filtros para facilitar a busca. Após importar a Collection para o ambiente de Test, é necessário executar a ação Forward para disponibilizá-la para importação no PRD.


Passo 1
Acessar app Export Software Collection



Criar uma Software Collection (caso ainda não tenha referente ao tema ou como foi modularizado/organizado no ambiente)


Nomear sua Software Collection e clicar em Create

Software Collection criada. Repare que ela é versionada, ou seja, a cada mudança de objeto e transporte, a mesma cria uma nova versão para transporte


Em Add Items, podemos adicionar os objetos que queremos incluir nessa Collection para transporte



Filtros disponibilizados de apoio para pesquisa no ambiente



Após inserir os objetos desejados, podemos clicar em Check para verificar se há algum impeditivo, dependência ou inconsistência para transporte

 

Opção Check
OBS: Temos a opção de transportar somente o que foi alterado, ou todos os itens novamente. Recomenda-se sempre a primeira opção



Ao final é gerado uma log com o status do transporte



Opção Export – É onde de fato acontece a liberação (Export) para o ambiente de Test

OBS: Temos a opção de transportar somente o que foi alterado, ou todos os itens novamente. Recomenda-se sempre a primeira opção.


Podemos inserir um comentário referente ao transporte a cada versionamento


Ao final é gerado uma log com o status do transporte

 


Passo 2
Após exportado, logar no ambiente de Test, e acessar app Import Collection


Podemos pesquisar pelos seguintes filtros. Ao chegar no ambiente de Test, a collection do versionamento em questão estará com o status “Ready for Import”

 

Selecionar a collection do versionamento desejado e selecionar a opção Check and Import



Adicionar comentário de transporte


Clicar em Manual Import



Importação iniciada:



O status da collection mudará para Importing durante o processamento:

Ao finalizar, a mensagem

 

Passo 3
Com isso, a collection foi apenas importada para o ambiente de Test, mas não necessariamente disponibilizada para a importação ao ambiente Produtivo. Para disponibilizarmos a ser importada a PRD, precisamos fazer o Forward dela:



Selecionar collection e clicar em Forward


Collection encaminhada à trilha do ambiente produtivo



Para finalizar, logar em ambiente produtivo e acessar app Import Collection


  

Selecionar a collection que deseja importar



Collection importada em PRD
